{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bad1ce3a621049d99e5a5f80","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Test Patterns Guide\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis guide explains the patterns and best practices for adding new error test cases to the ARMOR test suite. It provides step-by-step instructions for common scenarios and helps you choose the right approach for your testing needs.\n6\t\n7\t## Choosing Your Testing Approach\n8\t\n9\tBefore writing a test, decide which approach best fits your scenario:\n10\t\n11\t### When to Use **Fixtures**\n12\t- Testing standard HTTP errors (404, 500, etc.)\n13\t- Need reusable error responses\n14\t- Want consistent error structures across tests\n15\t- Testing against pre-defined error scenarios\n16\t\n17\t### When to Use **Validation Helpers**\n18\t- Testing custom API responses\n19\t- Need fine-grained control over validation\n20\t- Testing live endpoints\n21\t- Want to validate specific fields or patterns\n22\t\n23\t### When to Use **Test Tables**\n24\t- Testing multiple similar scenarios\n25\t- Data-driven testing with variations\n26\t- Need to test the same logic with different inputs\n27\t- Want organized, maintainable test cases\n28\t\n29\t## Pattern 1: Testing Standard Error Responses\n30\t\n31\t### Scenario: Testing a 404 Not Found Error\n32\t\n33\tUse this pattern when testing standard HTTP errors.\n34\t\n35\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n36\t\n37\t**1. Import the necessary fixtures and helpers:**\n38\t\n39\t```python\n40\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\n41\tfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status, validate_json_content_type\n42\tfrom tests.test_error_response_validation import validate_standard_error_response\n43\t```\n44\t\n45\t**2. Create the fixture with your test data:**\n46\t\n47\t```python\n48\tdef test_user_not_found():\n49\t    \"\"\"Test 404 response when user doesn't exist.\"\"\"\n50\t    # Create a 404 fixture for the specific endpoint\n51\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n52\t```\n53\t\n54\t**3. Validate the response:**\n55\t\n56\t```python\n57\t    # Validate status code\n58\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n59\t    \n60\t    # Validate content type\n61\t    validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n62\t    \n63\t    # Validate error structure\n64\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n65\t    \n66\t    # Validate specific fields\n67\t    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n68\t    assert 'not found' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n69\t```\n70\t\n71\t**4. Run the test:**\n72\t\n73\t```bash\n74\tpytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n75\t```\n76\t\n77\t### Complete Example\n78\t\n79\t```python\n80\tdef test_user_not_found():\n81\t    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found response for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n82\t    # Create fixture\n83\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n84\t    \n85\t    # Convert to tuple for validation\n86\t    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n87\t    \n88\t    # Validate status code\n89\t    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n90\t    \n91\t    # Validate content type\n92\t    validate_json_content_type(response)\n93\t    \n94\t    # Validate error structure\n95\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n96\t    \n97\t    # Validate specific fields\n98\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'not_found'\n99\t    assert fixture.body['code'] == 404\n100\t    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n101\t    assert 'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower() or '999' in fixture.body['message']\n102\t```\n103\t\n104\t## Pattern 2: Testing Custom Error Responses\n105\t\n106\t### Scenario: Testing a Custom Validation Error\n107\t\n108\tUse this pattern when you need to test errors specific to your API.\n109\t\n110\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n111\t\n112\t**1. Import the create_error_response builder:**\n113\t\n114\t```python\n115\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\n116\tfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t**2. Define your custom error structure:**\n120\t\n121\t```python\n122\tdef test_email_validation_error():\n123\t    \"\"\"Test custom email validation error.\"\"\"\n124\t    # Create a custom error response\n125\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n126\t        status=400,\n127\t        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n128\t        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n129\t        code=400,\n130\t        details={\n131\t            'field': 'email',\n132\t            'value': 'invalid-email',\n133\t            'error': 'Invalid email format'\n134\t        }\n135\t    )\n136\t```\n137\t\n138\t**3. Validate your custom error:**\n139\t\n140\t```python\n141\t    # Validate status code\n142\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n143\t    \n144\t    # Validate custom fields\n145\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n146\t    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n147\t    assert 'invalid' in fixture.body['details']['value']\n148\t```\n149\t\n150\t### Complete Example\n151\t\n152\t```python\n153\tdef test_email_validation_error():\n154\t    \"\"\"Test validation error for invalid email format.\"\"\"\n155\t    # Create custom validation error\n156\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n157\t        status=400,\n158\t        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n159\t        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n160\t        code=400,\n161\t        details={\n162\t            'field': 'email',\n163\t            'provided_value': 'not-an-email',\n164\t            'constraint': 'must be valid email format',\n165\t            'example': 'user@example.com'\n166\t        }\n167\t    )\n168\t    \n169\t    # Basic validation\n170\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n171\t    \n172\t    # Custom field validation\n173\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n174\t    assert 'email' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n175\t    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n176\t    assert '@' not in fixture.body['details']['provided_value']\n177\t    assert 'example.com' in fixture.body['details']['example']\n178\t```\n179\t\n180\t## Pattern 3: Testing Multiple Similar Scenarios\n181\t\n182\t### Scenario: Testing 404 Errors Across Multiple Endpoints\n183\t\n184\tUse this pattern when you have multiple similar test cases.\n185\t\n186\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n187\t\n188\t**1. Import test table components:**\n189\t\n190\t```python\n191\tfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase, create_not_found_test_table, run_test_table\n192\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\n193\t```\n194\t\n195\t**2. Define your test cases:**\n196\t\n197\t```python\n198\tdef test_multiple_404_scenarios():\n199\t    \"\"\"Test 404 responses across different endpoints.\"\"\"\n200\t    # Define test cases\n201\t    test_cases = [\n202\t        TestCase(\n203\t            name=\"User not found\",\n204\t            path=\"/api/users/999\",\n205\t            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n206\t        ),\n207\t        TestCase(\n208\t            name=\"Item not found\",\n209\t            path=\"/api/items/abc\",\n210\t            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n211\t        ),\n212\t        TestCase(\n213\t            name=\"Order not found\",\n214\t            path=\"/api/orders/xyz\",\n215\t            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n216\t        ),\n217\t    ]\n218\t```\n219\t\n220\t**3. Create and run the test table:**\n221\t\n222\t```python\n223\t    # Create test table\n224\t    table = create_not_found_test_table(test_cases)\n225\t    \n226\t    # Run all test cases\n227\t    results = []\n228\t    for case in test_cases:\n229\t        # Create fixture for each case\n230\t        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=case.path)\n231\t        \n232\t        # Validate\n233\t        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n234\t        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n235\t        \n236\t        results.append(True)\n237\t    \n238\t    # Assert all passed\n239\t    assert all(results)\n240\t```\n241\t\n242\t### Complete Example\n243\t\n244\t```python\n245\tdef test_multiple_not_found_scenarios():\n246\t    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found for various resource types.\"\"\"\n247\t    # Define test cases\n248\t    scenarios = [\n249\t        (\"/api/users/999\", \"user\"),\n250\t        (\"/api/items/abc\", \"item\"),\n251\t        (\"/api/orders/xyz\", \"order\"),\n252\t        (\"/api/products/invalid\", \"product\"),\n253\t    ]\n254\t    \n255\t    # Test each scenario\n256\t    for path, resource_type in scenarios:\n257\t        # Create fixture\n258\t        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=path)\n259\t        \n260\t        # Validate structure\n261\t        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n262\t        validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n263\t        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n264\t        \n265\t        # Validate path is in error details\n266\t        assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == path\n267\t        \n268\t        # Validate resource type is mentioned (optional)\n269\t        # assert resource_type in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n270\t```\n271\t\n272\t## Pattern 4: Testing Live Endpoints\n273\t\n274\t### Scenario: Testing a Real API Endpoint\n275\t\n276\tUse this pattern when testing against a running server.\n277\t\n278\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n279\t\n280\t**1. Import HTTP request helper:**\n281\t\n282\t```python\n283\tfrom tests.test_helpers import make_http_request, validate_http_status\n284\t```\n285\t\n286\t**2. Make the HTTP request:**\n287\t\n288\t```python\n289\tdef test_live_endpoint():\n290\t    \"\"\"Test a live API endpoint.\"\"\"\n291\t    # Make request to test server\n292\t    response = make_http_request(\n293\t        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n294\t        path='/api/users/999',\n295\t        method='GET'\n296\t    )\n297\t```\n298\t\n299\t**3. Validate the response:**\n300\t\n301\t```python\n302\t    # Check if request succeeded\n303\t    if not response['success']:\n304\t        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n305\t    \n306\t    # Validate status code\n307\t    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n308\t    \n309\t    # Parse and validate body\n310\t    import json\n311\t    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n312\t    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n313\t```\n314\t\n315\t### Complete Example\n316\t\n317\t```python\n318\t@pytest.mark.integration\n319\tdef test_live_user_endpoint():\n320\t    \"\"\"Test live user endpoint returns proper error for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n321\t    # Make request\n322\t    response = make_http_request(\n323\t        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n324\t        path='/api/users/999',\n325\t        method='GET',\n326\t        timeout=5\n327\t    )\n328\t    \n329\t    # Skip if server not available\n330\t    if not response['success']:\n331\t        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n332\t    \n333\t    # Validate response\n334\t    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n335\t    validate_json_content_type((response['status_code'], response['headers'], ''))\n336\t    \n337\t    # Parse and validate body\n338\t    import json\n339\t    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n340\t    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n341\t    \n342\t    # Validate specific fields\n343\t    assert body['error'] == 'not_found'\n344\t    assert body['code'] == 404\n345\t    assert body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n346\t```\n347\t\n348\t## Pattern 5: Testing HTTP Methods\n349\t\n350\t### Scenario: Testing Method Not Allowed Errors\n351\t\n352\tUse this pattern when testing HTTP method restrictions.\n353\t\n354\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n355\t\n356\t```python\n357\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import method_not_allowed_fixture\n358\t\n359\tdef test_method_not_allowed():\n360\t    \"\"\"Test 405 Method Not Allowed for DELETE on read-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n361\t    # Create 405 fixture\n362\t    fixture = method_not_allowed_fixture(\n363\t        path=\"/api/stats\",\n364\t        method=\"DELETE\",\n365\t        allowed_methods=[\"GET\", \"HEAD\", \"OPTIONS\"]\n366\t    )\n367\t    \n368\t    # Validate status code\n369\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 405)\n370\t    \n371\t    # Validate Allow header is present\n372\t    assert 'Allow' in fixture.headers\n373\t    assert 'GET' in fixture.headers['Allow']\n374\t    \n375\t    # Validate error body\n376\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n377\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'method_not_allowed'\n378\t    assert fixture.body['details']['method'] == 'DELETE'\n379\t```\n380\t\n381\t## Pattern 6: Testing Content-Type Errors\n382\t\n383\t### Scenario: Testing Unsupported Media Type Errors\n384\t\n385\tUse this pattern when testing content-type validation.\n386\t\n387\t```python\n388\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import unsupported_media_type_fixture\n389\t\n390\tdef test_unsupported_media_type():\n391\t    \"\"\"Test 415 Unsupported Media Type for XML request to JSON-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n392\t    # Create 415 fixture\n393\t    fixture = unsupported_media_type_fixture(\n394\t        path=\"/api/users\",\n395\t        content_type=\"application/xml\",\n396\t        supported_types=[\"application/json\"]\n397\t    )\n398\t    \n399\t    # Validate status code\n400\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 415)\n401\t    \n402\t    # Validate error details\n403\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n404\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unsupported_media_type'\n405\t    assert fixture.body['details']['provided_type'] == 'application/xml'\n406\t    assert fixture.body['details']['supported_types'] == ['application/json']\n407\t```\n408\t\n409\t## Pattern 7: Testing Server Errors\n410\t\n411\t### Scenario: Testing Internal Server Error Responses\n412\t\n413\tUse this pattern when testing server error handling.\n414\t\n415\t```python\n416\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import internal_server_error_fixture\n417\t\n418\tdef test_internal_server_error():\n419\t    \"\"\"Test 500 Internal Server Error response.\"\"\"\n420\t    # Create 500 fixture\n421\t    fixture = internal_server_error_fixture(\n422\t        path=\"/api/users\",\n423\t        error_id=\"ERR-DB-001\"\n424\t    )\n425\t    \n426\t    # Validate status code\n427\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 500)\n428\t    \n429\t    # Validate error structure\n430\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n431\t    \n432\t    # Validate server error specifics\n433\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'internal_server_error'\n434\t    assert 'error_id' in fixture.body['details']\n435\t    assert fixture.headers.get('X-Error-ID') == 'ERR-DB-001'\n436\t```\n437\t\n438\t## Pattern 8: Testing CORS Headers\n439\t\n440\t### Scenario: Testing CORS Configuration\n441\t\n442\tUse this pattern when testing CORS headers.\n443\t\n444\t```python\n445\tfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_cors_headers\n446\t\n447\tdef test_cors_headers():\n448\t    \"\"\"Test CORS headers are properly configured.\"\"\"\n449\t    # Create response with CORS headers\n450\t    response = (\n451\t        200,\n452\t        {\n453\t            'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'https://example.com',\n454\t            'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',\n455\t            'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials': 'true'\n456\t        },\n457\t        '{}'\n458\t    )\n459\t    \n460\t    # Validate CORS headers\n461\t    validate_cors_headers(\n462\t        response,\n463\t        expected_origin='https://example.com'\n464\t    )\n465\t```\n466\t\n467\t## Pattern 9: Testing Authentication Errors\n468\t\n469\t### Scenario: Testing Authentication/Authorization Errors\n470\t\n471\tUse this pattern when testing auth failures.\n472\t\n473\t```python\n474\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\n475\t\n476\tdef test_authentication_error():\n477\t    \"\"\"Test 401 Unauthorized response for missing credentials.\"\"\"\n478\t    # Create 401 fixture\n479\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n480\t        status=401,\n481\t        error_type=\"unauthorized\",\n482\t        message=\"Authentication required\",\n483\t        code=401,\n484\t        details={\n485\t            'auth_type': 'Bearer',\n486\t            'realm': 'api'\n487\t        }\n488\t    )\n489\t    \n490\t    # Validate\n491\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 401)\n492\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n493\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unauthorized'\n494\t```\n495\t\n496\t## Pattern 10: Batch Testing Multiple Fixtures\n497\t\n498\t### Scenario: Testing All Fixtures in a Category\n499\t\n500\tUse this pattern to validate all fixtures of a certain type.\n501\t\n502\t```python\n503\tdef test_all_client_error_fixtures():\n504\t    \"\"\"Test all client error fixtures are valid.\"\"\"\n505\t    # Get all client error fixtures\n506\t    from tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES\n507\t    \n508\t    # Test each fixture\n509\t    for name, fixture in CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES.items():\n510\t        # Validate status code is in client error range\n511\t        assert 400 <= fixture.status_code < 500, \\\n512\t            f\"{name} has invalid status code {fixture.status_code}\"\n513\t        \n514\t        # Validate error structure\n515\t        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n516\t        \n517\t        print(f\"✓ {name} validated\")\n518\t```\n519\t\n520\t## Best Practices\n521\t\n522\t### 1. Test Structure\n523\t\n524\tOrganize your tests logically:\n525\t\n526\t```python\n527\tclass TestUserErrors:\n528\t    \"\"\"Tests for user-related error responses.\"\"\"\n529\t    \n530\t    def test_user_not_found(self):\n531\t        \"\"\"Test 404 for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n532\t        pass\n533\t    \n534\t    def test_user_validation_error(self):\n535\t        \"\"\"Test 400 for invalid user data.\"\"\"\n536\t        pass\n537\t    \n538\t    def test_user_unauthorized(self):\n539\t        \"\"\"Test 401 for unauthorized access.\"\"\"\n540\t        pass\n541\t```\n542\t\n543\t### 2. Descriptive Test Names\n544\t\n545\tUse clear, descriptive test names:\n546\t\n547\t```python\n548\t# Good\n549\tdef test_user_not_found_returns_404_with_path_in_details():\n550\t    \"\"\"Test that requesting non-existent user returns 404 with path in error details.\"\"\"\n551\t    pass\n552\t\n553\t# Less clear\n554\tdef test_error():\n555\t    pass\n556\t```\n557\t\n558\t### 3. Validation Order\n559\t\n560\tValidate in a logical order:\n561\t\n562\t```python\n563\tdef test_validation_order():\n564\t    \"\"\"Demonstrate proper validation order.\"\"\"\n565\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n566\t    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n567\t    \n568\t    # 1. Validate status code (fastest check)\n569\t    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n570\t    \n571\t    # 2. Validate content type (medium check)\n572\t    validate_json_content_type(response)\n573\t    \n574\t    # 3. Validate error structure (slower check)\n575\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n576\t    \n577\t    # 4. Validate specific fields (most specific)\n578\t    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/test'\n579\t```\n580\t\n581\t### 4. Error Messages\n582\t\n583\tUse assertion messages for clarity:\n584\t\n585\t```python\n586\tdef test_with_clear_messages():\n587\t    \"\"\"Test with clear assertion messages.\"\"\"\n588\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n589\t    \n590\t    # Clear assertion messages\n591\t    assert fixture.status_code == 404, \\\n592\t        f\"Expected 404, got {fixture.status_code}\"\n593\t    \n594\t    assert 'path' in fixture.body['details'], \\\n595\t        \"Error details should include 'path' field\"\n596\t```\n597\t\n598\t### 5. Test Independence\n599\t\n600\tEnsure tests don't depend on each other:\n601\t\n602\t```python\n603\t# Good - each test is independent\n604\tdef test_scenario_1():\n605\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test1\")\n606\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n607\t\n608\tdef test_scenario_2():\n609\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test2\")\n610\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n611\t\n612\t# Bad - test_scenario_2 depends on test_scenario_1\n613\tshared_fixture = None\n614\t\n615\tdef test_scenario_1():\n616\t    global shared_fixture\n617\t    shared_fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n618\t\n619\tdef test_scenario_2():\n620\t    validate_http_status(shared_fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n621\t```\n622\t\n623\t## Common Testing Scenarios\n624\t\n625\t### Scenario 1: Testing a New Endpoint\n626\t\n627\t```python\n628\tdef test_new_endpoint():\n629\t    \"\"\"Test a new endpoint returns proper error for invalid input.\"\"\"\n630\t    # Create fixture for your endpoint\n631\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n632\t        status=400,\n633\t        error_type=\"invalid_input\",\n634\t        message=\"Invalid input parameter\",\n635\t        code=400,\n636\t        details={'parameter': 'sort', 'value': 'invalid'}\n637\t    )\n638\t    \n639\t    # Validate\n640\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n641\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n642\t```\n643\t\n644\t### Scenario 2: Testing Error Messages\n645\t\n646\t```python\n647\tdef test_error_message_content():\n648\t    \"\"\"Test that error messages are user-friendly.\"\"\"\n649\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n650\t    \n651\t    # Check message contains helpful information\n652\t    assert 'users/999' in fixture.body['message'] or \\\n653\t           'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n654\t    \n655\t    # Check message is not too technical\n656\t    assert 'exception' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n657\t    assert 'traceback' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n658\t```\n659\t\n660\t### Scenario 3: Testing Error Details\n661\t\n662\t```python\n663\tdef test_error_details_completeness():\n664\t    \"\"\"Test that error details include relevant information.\"\"\"\n665\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n666\t    \n667\t    # Check required detail fields\n668\t    details = fixture.body['details']\n669\t    assert 'path' in details\n670\t    assert details['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n671\t```\n672\t\n673\t## Running Tests\n674\t\n675\t### Run All Tests\n676\t\n677\t```bash\n678\tpytest tests/ -v\n679\t```\n680\t\n681\t### Run Specific Test File\n682\t\n683\t```bash\n684\tpytest tests/test_my_errors.py -v\n685\t```\n686\t\n687\t### Run Specific Test\n688\t\n689\t```bash\n690\tpytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n691\t```\n692\t\n693\t### Run with Coverage\n694\t\n695\t```bash\n696\tpytest tests/ --cov=tests --cov-report=html\n697\t```\n698\t\n699\t## Troubleshooting\n700\t\n701\t### Common Issues\n702\t\n703\t**1. Import Errors**\n704\t\n705\t```bash\n706\t# Error: ImportError: No module named 'tests.fixtures'\n707\t# Solution: Ensure you're running from the project root\n708\tcd /home/coding/ARMOR\n709\tpytest tests/\n710\t```\n711\t\n712\t**2. Fixture Not Found**\n713\t\n714\t```python\n715\t# Error: get_fixture() returns None\n716\t# Solution: Check fixture name spelling and availability\n717\tnames = list_fixtures()\n718\tprint(names)  # Check if your fixture exists\n719\t```\n720\t\n721\t**3. Validation Failures**\n722\t\n723\t```python\n724\t# Error: StatusValidationError\n725\t# Solution: Check your expected vs actual status codes\n726\ttry:\n727\t    validate_http_status(response, 200)\n728\texcept StatusValidationError as e:\n729\t    print(f\"Expected 200, got {e.actual}\")\n730\t```\n731\t\n732\t## Adding New Fixtures\n733\t\n734\tIf you need a new fixture type:\n735\t\n736\t1. **Add to `fixtures/error_scenarios.py`:**\n737\t\n738\t```python\n739\tdef conflict_fixture(path: str, message: str = None) -> ErrorResponseFixture:\n740\t    \"\"\"Create a 409 Conflict error fixture.\"\"\"\n741\t    return create_error_response(\n742\t        status=409,\n743\t        error_type=\"conflict\",\n744\t        message=message or f\"Resource conflict at {path}\",\n745\t        code=409,\n746\t        details={'path': path, 'conflict_type': 'resource_exists'}\n747\t    )\n748\t```\n749\t\n750\t2. **Export from `__init__.py`:**\n751\t\n752\t```python\n753\tfrom .error_scenarios import conflict_fixture\n754\t```\n755\t\n756\t3. **Add to collection (if applicable):**\n757\t\n758\t```python\n759\tALL_ERROR_FIXTURES['409_conflict'] = conflict_fixture()\n760\t```\n761\t\n762\t## Next Steps\n763\t\n764\t- Review [README.md](README.md) for infrastructure overview\n765\t- Check [example_test.py](example_test.py) for runnable examples\n766\t- See [fixtures/README.md](fixtures/README.md) for fixture documentation\n767\t- Reference [TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md](TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md) for test table usage\n768\t\n769\tBead: bf-qxhx20\n770\tCreated: 2026-07-15\n771\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6ac7b4eb-a3e0-4051-b492-413d1488a600","uuid":"368534f3-6b55-4625-ae55-67a711ea0360","timestamp":"2026-07-15T18:14:40.840Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/tests/TEST_PATTERNS.md","content":"# Test Patterns Guide\n\n## Overview\n\nThis guide explains the patterns and best practices for adding new error test cases to the ARMOR test suite. It provides step-by-step instructions for common scenarios and helps you choose the right approach for your testing needs.\n\n## Choosing Your Testing Approach\n\nBefore writing a test, decide which approach best fits your scenario:\n\n### When to Use **Fixtures**\n- Testing standard HTTP errors (404, 500, etc.)\n- Need reusable error responses\n- Want consistent error structures across tests\n- Testing against pre-defined error scenarios\n\n### When to Use **Validation Helpers**\n- Testing custom API responses\n- Need fine-grained control over validation\n- Testing live endpoints\n- Want to validate specific fields or patterns\n\n### When to Use **Test Tables**\n- Testing multiple similar scenarios\n- Data-driven testing with variations\n- Need to test the same logic with different inputs\n- Want organized, maintainable test cases\n\n## Pattern 1: Testing Standard Error Responses\n\n### Scenario: Testing a 404 Not Found Error\n\nUse this pattern when testing standard HTTP errors.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import the necessary fixtures and helpers:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\nfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status, validate_json_content_type\nfrom tests.test_error_response_validation import validate_standard_error_response\n```\n\n**2. Create the fixture with your test data:**\n\n```python\ndef test_user_not_found():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 response when user doesn't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Create a 404 fixture for the specific endpoint\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n```\n\n**3. Validate the response:**\n\n```python\n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n    \n    # Validate content type\n    validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n    \n    # Validate error structure\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # Validate specific fields\n    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n    assert 'not found' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n```\n\n**4. Run the test:**\n\n```bash\npytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\ndef test_user_not_found():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found response for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n    # Create fixture\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n    \n    # Convert to tuple for validation\n    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n    \n    # Validate content type\n    validate_json_content_type(response)\n    \n    # Validate error structure\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # Validate specific fields\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'not_found'\n    assert fixture.body['code'] == 404\n    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n    assert 'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower() or '999' in fixture.body['message']\n```\n\n## Pattern 2: Testing Custom Error Responses\n\n### Scenario: Testing a Custom Validation Error\n\nUse this pattern when you need to test errors specific to your API.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import the create_error_response builder:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\nfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status\n```\n\n**2. Define your custom error structure:**\n\n```python\ndef test_email_validation_error():\n    \"\"\"Test custom email validation error.\"\"\"\n    # Create a custom error response\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=400,\n        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n        code=400,\n        details={\n            'field': 'email',\n            'value': 'invalid-email',\n            'error': 'Invalid email format'\n        }\n    )\n```\n\n**3. Validate your custom error:**\n\n```python\n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n    \n    # Validate custom fields\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n    assert 'invalid' in fixture.body['details']['value']\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\ndef test_email_validation_error():\n    \"\"\"Test validation error for invalid email format.\"\"\"\n    # Create custom validation error\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=400,\n        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n        code=400,\n        details={\n            'field': 'email',\n            'provided_value': 'not-an-email',\n            'constraint': 'must be valid email format',\n            'example': 'user@example.com'\n        }\n    )\n    \n    # Basic validation\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n    \n    # Custom field validation\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n    assert 'email' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n    assert '@' not in fixture.body['details']['provided_value']\n    assert 'example.com' in fixture.body['details']['example']\n```\n\n## Pattern 3: Testing Multiple Similar Scenarios\n\n### Scenario: Testing 404 Errors Across Multiple Endpoints\n\nUse this pattern when you have multiple similar test cases.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import test table components:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase, create_not_found_test_table, run_test_table\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\n```\n\n**2. Define your test cases:**\n\n```python\ndef test_multiple_404_scenarios():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 responses across different endpoints.\"\"\"\n    # Define test cases\n    test_cases = [\n        TestCase(\n            name=\"User not found\",\n            path=\"/api/users/999\",\n            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            name=\"Item not found\",\n            path=\"/api/items/abc\",\n            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            name=\"Order not found\",\n            path=\"/api/orders/xyz\",\n            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n        ),\n    ]\n```\n\n**3. Create and run the test table:**\n\n```python\n    # Create test table\n    table = create_not_found_test_table(test_cases)\n    \n    # Run all test cases\n    results = []\n    for case in test_cases:\n        # Create fixture for each case\n        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=case.path)\n        \n        # Validate\n        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n        \n        results.append(True)\n    \n    # Assert all passed\n    assert all(results)\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\ndef test_multiple_not_found_scenarios():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found for various resource types.\"\"\"\n    # Define test cases\n    scenarios = [\n        (\"/api/users/999\", \"user\"),\n        (\"/api/items/abc\", \"item\"),\n        (\"/api/orders/xyz\", \"order\"),\n        (\"/api/products/invalid\", \"product\"),\n    ]\n    \n    # Test each scenario\n    for path, resource_type in scenarios:\n        # Create fixture\n        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=path)\n        \n        # Validate structure\n        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n        validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n        \n        # Validate path is in error details\n        assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == path\n        \n        # Validate resource type is mentioned (optional)\n        # assert resource_type in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n```\n\n## Pattern 4: Testing Live Endpoints\n\n### Scenario: Testing a Real API Endpoint\n\nUse this pattern when testing against a running server.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import HTTP request helper:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_helpers import make_http_request, validate_http_status\n```\n\n**2. Make the HTTP request:**\n\n```python\ndef test_live_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Test a live API endpoint.\"\"\"\n    # Make request to test server\n    response = make_http_request(\n        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n        path='/api/users/999',\n        method='GET'\n    )\n```\n\n**3. Validate the response:**\n\n```python\n    # Check if request succeeded\n    if not response['success']:\n        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n    \n    # Parse and validate body\n    import json\n    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\n@pytest.mark.integration\ndef test_live_user_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Test live user endpoint returns proper error for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n    # Make request\n    response = make_http_request(\n        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n        path='/api/users/999',\n        method='GET',\n        timeout=5\n    )\n    \n    # Skip if server not available\n    if not response['success']:\n        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n    \n    # Validate response\n    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n    validate_json_content_type((response['status_code'], response['headers'], ''))\n    \n    # Parse and validate body\n    import json\n    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n    \n    # Validate specific fields\n    assert body['error'] == 'not_found'\n    assert body['code'] == 404\n    assert body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n```\n\n## Pattern 5: Testing HTTP Methods\n\n### Scenario: Testing Method Not Allowed Errors\n\nUse this pattern when testing HTTP method restrictions.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import method_not_allowed_fixture\n\ndef test_method_not_allowed():\n    \"\"\"Test 405 Method Not Allowed for DELETE on read-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n    # Create 405 fixture\n    fixture = method_not_allowed_fixture(\n        path=\"/api/stats\",\n        method=\"DELETE\",\n        allowed_methods=[\"GET\", \"HEAD\", \"OPTIONS\"]\n    )\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 405)\n    \n    # Validate Allow header is present\n    assert 'Allow' in fixture.headers\n    assert 'GET' in fixture.headers['Allow']\n    \n    # Validate error body\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'method_not_allowed'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['method'] == 'DELETE'\n```\n\n## Pattern 6: Testing Content-Type Errors\n\n### Scenario: Testing Unsupported Media Type Errors\n\nUse this pattern when testing content-type validation.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import unsupported_media_type_fixture\n\ndef test_unsupported_media_type():\n    \"\"\"Test 415 Unsupported Media Type for XML request to JSON-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n    # Create 415 fixture\n    fixture = unsupported_media_type_fixture(\n        path=\"/api/users\",\n        content_type=\"application/xml\",\n        supported_types=[\"application/json\"]\n    )\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 415)\n    \n    # Validate error details\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unsupported_media_type'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['provided_type'] == 'application/xml'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['supported_types'] == ['application/json']\n```\n\n## Pattern 7: Testing Server Errors\n\n### Scenario: Testing Internal Server Error Responses\n\nUse this pattern when testing server error handling.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import internal_server_error_fixture\n\ndef test_internal_server_error():\n    \"\"\"Test 500 Internal Server Error response.\"\"\"\n    # Create 500 fixture\n    fixture = internal_server_error_fixture(\n        path=\"/api/users\",\n        error_id=\"ERR-DB-001\"\n    )\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 500)\n    \n    # Validate error structure\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # Validate server error specifics\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'internal_server_error'\n    assert 'error_id' in fixture.body['details']\n    assert fixture.headers.get('X-Error-ID') == 'ERR-DB-001'\n```\n\n## Pattern 8: Testing CORS Headers\n\n### Scenario: Testing CORS Configuration\n\nUse this pattern when testing CORS headers.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_cors_headers\n\ndef test_cors_headers():\n    \"\"\"Test CORS headers are properly configured.\"\"\"\n    # Create response with CORS headers\n    response = (\n        200,\n        {\n            'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'https://example.com',\n            'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',\n            'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials': 'true'\n        },\n        '{}'\n    )\n    \n    # Validate CORS headers\n    validate_cors_headers(\n        response,\n        expected_origin='https://example.com'\n    )\n```\n\n## Pattern 9: Testing Authentication Errors\n\n### Scenario: Testing Authentication/Authorization Errors\n\nUse this pattern when testing auth failures.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\n\ndef test_authentication_error():\n    \"\"\"Test 401 Unauthorized response for missing credentials.\"\"\"\n    # Create 401 fixture\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=401,\n        error_type=\"unauthorized\",\n        message=\"Authentication required\",\n        code=401,\n        details={\n            'auth_type': 'Bearer',\n            'realm': 'api'\n        }\n    )\n    \n    # Validate\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 401)\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unauthorized'\n```\n\n## Pattern 10: Batch Testing Multiple Fixtures\n\n### Scenario: Testing All Fixtures in a Category\n\nUse this pattern to validate all fixtures of a certain type.\n\n```python\ndef test_all_client_error_fixtures():\n    \"\"\"Test all client error fixtures are valid.\"\"\"\n    # Get all client error fixtures\n    from tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES\n    \n    # Test each fixture\n    for name, fixture in CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES.items():\n        # Validate status code is in client error range\n        assert 400 <= fixture.status_code < 500, \\\n            f\"{name} has invalid status code {fixture.status_code}\"\n        \n        # Validate error structure\n        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n        \n        print(f\"✓ {name} validated\")\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Test Structure\n\nOrganize your tests logically:\n\n```python\nclass TestUserErrors:\n    \"\"\"Tests for user-related error responses.\"\"\"\n    \n    def test_user_not_found(self):\n        \"\"\"Test 404 for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n        pass\n    \n    def test_user_validation_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Test 400 for invalid user data.\"\"\"\n        pass\n    \n    def test_user_unauthorized(self):\n        \"\"\"Test 401 for unauthorized access.\"\"\"\n        pass\n```\n\n### 2. Descriptive Test Names\n\nUse clear, descriptive test names:\n\n```python\n# Good\ndef test_user_not_found_returns_404_with_path_in_details():\n    \"\"\"Test that requesting non-existent user returns 404 with path in error details.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n# Less clear\ndef test_error():\n    pass\n```\n\n### 3. Validation Order\n\nValidate in a logical order:\n\n```python\ndef test_validation_order():\n    \"\"\"Demonstrate proper validation order.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n    \n    # 1. Validate status code (fastest check)\n    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n    \n    # 2. Validate content type (medium check)\n    validate_json_content_type(response)\n    \n    # 3. Validate error structure (slower check)\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # 4. Validate specific fields (most specific)\n    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/test'\n```\n\n### 4. Error Messages\n\nUse assertion messages for clarity:\n\n```python\ndef test_with_clear_messages():\n    \"\"\"Test with clear assertion messages.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n    \n    # Clear assertion messages\n    assert fixture.status_code == 404, \\\n        f\"Expected 404, got {fixture.status_code}\"\n    \n    assert 'path' in fixture.body['details'], \\\n        \"Error details should include 'path' field\"\n```\n\n### 5. Test Independence\n\nEnsure tests don't depend on each other:\n\n```python\n# Good - each test is independent\ndef test_scenario_1():\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test1\")\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n\ndef test_scenario_2():\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test2\")\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n\n# Bad - test_scenario_2 depends on test_scenario_1\nshared_fixture = None\n\ndef test_scenario_1():\n    global shared_fixture\n    shared_fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n\ndef test_scenario_2():\n    validate_http_status(shared_fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n```\n\n## Common Testing Scenarios\n\n### Scenario 1: Testing a New Endpoint\n\n```python\ndef test_new_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Test a new endpoint returns proper error for invalid input.\"\"\"\n    # Create fixture for your endpoint\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=400,\n        error_type=\"invalid_input\",\n        message=\"Invalid input parameter\",\n        code=400,\n        details={'parameter': 'sort', 'value': 'invalid'}\n    )\n    \n    # Validate\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n```\n\n### Scenario 2: Testing Error Messages\n\n```python\ndef test_error_message_content():\n    \"\"\"Test that error messages are user-friendly.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n    \n    # Check message contains helpful information\n    assert 'users/999' in fixture.body['message'] or \\\n           'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n    \n    # Check message is not too technical\n    assert 'exception' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n    assert 'traceback' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n```\n\n### Scenario 3: Testing Error Details\n\n```python\ndef test_error_details_completeness():\n    \"\"\"Test that error details include relevant information.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n    \n    # Check required detail fields\n    details = fixture.body['details']\n    assert 'path' in details\n    assert details['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\n### Run All Tests\n\n```bash\npytest tests/ -v\n```\n\n### Run Specific Test File\n\n```bash\npytest tests/test_my_errors.py -v\n```\n\n### Run Specific Test\n\n```bash\npytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n```\n\n### Run with Coverage\n\n```bash\npytest tests/ --cov=tests --cov-report=html\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Common Issues\n\n**1. Import Errors**\n\n```bash\n# Error: ImportError: No module named 'tests.fixtures'\n# Solution: Ensure you're running from the project root\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\npytest tests/\n```\n\n**2. infrastructure is organized into three main layers:\n10\t\n11\t### 1. **Validation Helpers** (`test_helpers.py`)\n12\t\n13\tLow-level validation functions for HTTP responses:\n14\t\n15\t- **Status Validation**: `validate_http_status()`, `validate_success()`, `validate_client_error()`, `validate_server_error()`\n16\t- **Content-Type Validation**: `validate_content_type()`, **HTTP Request Helper**: `make_http_request()` for making test requests with error handling\n19\t\n20\t### 2. **Error Response Fixtures** (`fixtures/error_scenarios.py`)\n21\t\n22\tPre-configured error response fixtures for common HTTP error scenarios:\n23\t\n24\t- **Client Errors (4xx)**: 400 Bad Request, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 405 Method Not Allowed, 415 Unsupported Media Type\n25\t- **Server Errors (5xx)**: 500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable\n26\t- **Generic Builders**: `create_error_response()`, `create_error_batch()` for custom authentication tests, validation tests\n33\t- **Table Builders**: `create_auth_test_table()`, `create_not_found_test_table()`, `create_rate_limit_test_table()`\n34\t- **Execution Helpers**: `run_test_case()`, `run_test_table()` for running tests\n35\t- **Extensible Architecture**: Easy to create custom test tables for any error type\n36\t- **Extension Examples**: Comprehensive examples for S3, database, network, and business logic errors\n37\t\n38\t## Key Design Decisions\n39\t\n40\t### Fixture Immutability\n41\t\n42\tError fixtures use immutable builder patterns:\n43\t\n44\t```python\n45\tfixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users\")\n46\t# Returns new fixture, doesn't modify original\n47\twith_path = fixture.with_path(\"/api/items\")\n48\t```\n49\t\n50\t**Why**: This prevents accidental state mutations in parallel tests and makes test behavior predictable.\n51\t\n52\t### Tuple-Based Response Format\n53\t\n54\tValidation functions accept both `requests.Response` objects and simple tuples:\n55\t\n56\t```python\n57\t# With requests.Response\n58\tvalidate_http_status(response, 200)\n59\t\n60\t# With tuple (status, headers, body)\n61\tvalidate_http_status((200, {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, '{}'), 200)\n62\t```\n63\t\n64\t**Why**: Tuples work without the `requests` library, making tests runnable in minimal environments and easier to mock.\n65\t\n66\t### Pattern-Matching Content Types\n67\t\n68\tContent-Type validation uses pattern matching:\n69\t\n70\t```python\n71\t# Both of these match 'application/json'\n72\tvalidate_content_type(response, 'application/json')\n73\tvalidate_content_type(response, 'application/json; charset=utf-8')\n74\t```\n75\t\n76\t**Why**: Real-world responses often include charset parameters. Pattern matching makes tests more robust without complex logic.\n77\t\n78\t### Collection-Based Fixtures\n79\t\n80\tFixtures are organized in collections by category:\n81\t\n82\t```python\n83\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES, SERVER_ERROR_FIXTURES\n84\t```\n85\t\n86\t**Why**: Enables bulk testing against all client errors or all server errors without maintaining manual lists.\n87\t\n88\t## Quick Start Examples\n89\t\n90\t### Validating HTTP Status Codes\n91\t\n92\t```python\n93\tfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status, validate_client_error\n94\t\n95\t# Single status code\n96\tvalidate_http_status(response, 200)\n97\t\n98\t# Multiple acceptable codes\n99\tvalidate_http_status(response, [200, 201, 204])\n100\t\n101\t# Check for client error\n102\tvalidate_client_error(response)\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t### Using Error Fixtures\n106\t\n107\t```python\n108\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture, get_fixture\n109\t\n110\t# Create a 404 fixture\n111\tfixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/123\")\n112\tassert fixture.status_code == 404\n113\t\n114\t# Get pre-configured fixture\n115\tfixture = get_fixture('404_not_found')\n116\tassert fixture.status_code == 404\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t### Making Test Requests\n120\t\n121\t```python\n122\tfrom tests.test_helpers import make_http_request\n123\t\n124\tresponse = make_http_request(\n125\t    base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n126\t    path='/api/health',\n127\t    method='GET'\n128\t)\n129\t\n130\tif response['success']:\n131\t    print(f\"Status: {response['status_code']}\")\n132\t    print(f\"Body: {response['body']}\")\n133\t```\n134\t\n135\t## Testing Patterns\n136\t\n137\t### Pattern 1: Fixture-Based Testing\n138\t\n139\tUse pre-configured fixtures for standard error scenarios:\n140\t\n141\t```python\n142\tdef test_404_not_found():\n143\t    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found response structure.\"\"\"\n144\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/123\")\n145\t    \n146\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n147\t    validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n148\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n149\t```\n150\t\n151\t### Pattern 2: Table-Based Testing\n152\t\n153\tUse test tables for data-driven scenarios:\n154\t\n155\t```python\n156\tdef test_not_found_multiple_paths():\n157\t    \"\"\"Test 404 response for multiple path types.\"\"\"\n158\t    table = create_not_found_test_table([\n159\t        TestCase(path=\"/api/users/999\", expected_code=\"not_found\"),\n160\t        TestCase(path=\"/api/items/abc\", expected_code=\"not_found\"),\n161\t        TestCase(path=\"/api/orders/xyz\", expected_code=\"not_found\"),\n162\t    ])\n163\t    \n164\t    results = run_test_table(table)\n165\t    assert all(result.passed for result in results)\n166\t```\n167\t\n168\t### Pattern 3: Helper-Based Validation\n169\t\n170\tUse validation helpers for custom test logic:\n171\t\n172\t```python\n173\tdef test_custom_error_response():\n174\t    \"\"\"Test custom error response with specific fields.\"\"\"\n175\t    response = make_http_request('http://localhost:8080', '/api/error')\n176\t    \n177\t    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 418)\n178\t    validate_content_type(response['headers'], 'application/json')\n179\t    \n180\t    # Custom validation\n181\t    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n182\t    assert body['error'] == 'custom_error'\n183\t    assert 'details' in body\n184\t```\n185\t\n186\t## Adding New Error Test Cases\n187\t\n188\tWhen adding new error test cases, follow these steps:\n189\t\n190\t1. **Choose Your Approach**:\n191\t   - Use existing fixtures for standard errors (404, 500, etc.)\n192\t   - Create custom fixtures with `create_error_response()` for new scenarios\n193\t   - Use test tables for multiple similar scenarios\n194\t\n195\t2. **Write the Test**:\n196\t   ```python\n197\t   def test_my_new_scenario():\n198\t       fixture = create_error_response(\n199\t           status=418,\n200\t           error_type=\"im_a_teapot\",\n201\t           message=\"I'm a teapot\",\n202\t           code=418\n203\t       )\n204\t       \n205\t       validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 418)\n206\t       validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n207\t   ```\n208\t\n209\t3. **Run and Verify**:\n210\t   ```bash\n211\t   pytest tests/test_my_new_scenario.py -v\n212\t   ```\n213\t\n214\tSee [TEST_PATTERNS.md](TEST_PATTERNS.md) for detailed guidance on adding new test cases.\n215\t\n216\t## Extending Test Tables\n217\t\n218\tThe test table framework is designed to be easily extensible for custom error types and scenarios. You can create specialized test tables for:\n219\t\n220\t- **Protocol-specific errors** (S3, WebSocket, gRPC)\n221\t- **Infrastructure errors** (database, network, filesystem)\n222\t- **Business logic errors** (validation, state transitions, constraints)\n223\t- **Domain-specific errors** (financial, healthcare, e-commerce)\n224\t\n225\t### Quick Example\n226\t\n227\t```python\n228\tfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase, ErrorTestTable\n229\t\n230\tdef create_s3_error_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n231\t    return ErrorTestTable(\n232\t        name=\"s3_errors\",\n233\t        description=\"S3 protocol-specific error scenarios\",\n234\t        test_cases=[\n235\t            TestCase(\n236\t                id=\"S3-001\",\n237\t                description=\"Bucket not found\",\n238\t                input_data={\"operation\": \"ListObjects\", \"bucket\": \"nonexistent\"},\n239\t                expected_status=404,\n240\t                expected_error=\"NoSuchBucket\",\n241\t                expected_message=\"The specified bucket does not exist\"\n242\t            )\n243\t        ]\n244\t    )\n245\t```\n246\t\n247\t### Extension Documentation\n248\t\n249\t- **[TEST_TABLE_EXTENSION_GUIDE.md](TEST_TABLE_EXTENSION_GUIDE.md)** - Comprehensive guide for creating custom test tables\n250\t- **[test_table_extensions_examples.py](test_table_extensions_examples.py)** - Complete working examples for various error types\n251\t- **[TEST_TABLE_QUICKSTART.md](TEST_TABLE_QUICKSTART.md)** - Quick reference for common patterns\n252\t\n253\t### Available Extension Examples\n254\t\n255\tThe `test_table_extensions_examples.py` file includes complete implementations for:\n256\t\n257\t1. **S3 Errors** - Bucket/object operations, ACL issues, multi-part upload errors\n258\t2. **Database Errors** - Connection issues, constraint violations, transaction deadlocks\n259\t3. **Rate Limiting** - Token buckets, sliding windows, per-user/per-IP limits\n260\t4. **Network Errors** - Timeouts, DNS failures, SSL/TLS errors, connection resets\n261\t5. **Filesystem Errors** - Permission issues, disk full, file not found\n262\t6. **Business Logic Errors** - State transitions, concurrent modification, rule violations\n263\t\n264\tEach example includes the test table definition and a demo executor function.\n265\t\n266\t## Module Organization\n267\t\n268\t```\n269\ttests/\n270\t├── README.md                           # This file\n271\t├── TEST_PATTERNS.md                   # Guide for adding new test cases\n272\t├── __init__.py                         # Package exports\n273\t├── example_test.py                     # Usage examples\n274\t├── test_helpers.py                     # Low-level validation helpers\n275\t├── test_error_response_validation.py  # Error response structure validation\n276\t├── test_tables.py                      # Test table management\n277\t├── test_http_validation_helpers.py    # HTTP-specific validation helpers\n278\t├── test_error_scenario_fixtures.py    # Tests for error fixtures\n279\t└── fixtures/\n280\t    ├── __init__.py                     # Fixture exports\n281\t    ├── error_scenarios.py              # Error response fixtures\n282\t    └── README.md                       # Fixture documentation\n283\t```\n284\t\n285\t## Exported Functions\n286\t\n287\tThe `tests` package exports the following public API:\n288\t\n289\t### From `test_helpers`:\n290\t- `validate_http_status()` - Validate HTTP status codes\n291\t- `validate_content_type()` - Validate Content-Type headers\n292\t- `validate_cors_headers()` - Validate CORS headers\n293\t- `make_http_request()` - Make HTTP requests with error handling\n294\t\n295\t### From `test_error_response_validation`:\n296\t- `validate_error_response()` - Validate error response structure\n297\t- `validate_standard_error_response()` - Validate standard error format\n298\t- `validate_detailed_error_response()` - Validate detailed error format\n299\t\n300\t### From `test_tables`:\n301\t- `TestCase` - Test case dataclass\n302\t- `TestTableResult` - Test table execution results\n303\t- `run_test_table()` - Execute a test table\n304\t- `create_simple_test_table()` - Create a simple test table\n305\t\n306\t## Related Documentation\n307\t\n308\t- [TEST_PATTERNS.md](TEST_PATTERNS.md) - \"Add documentation and usage examples\".\n325\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6ac7b4eb-a3e0-4051-b492-413d1488a600","uuid":"27a0719c-57e2-46ee-8b04-18f535dacf53","timestamp":"2026-07-15T18:14:44.147Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/tests/README.md","content":"# ARMOR Test Infrastructure\n\n## Overview\n\nThis package provides a comprehensive test infrastructure for validating ARMOR's HTTP error responses and API behaviors. It includes reusable helpers, pre-configured test fixtures, and structured test tables that make writing robust tests faster and more consistent.\n\n## Architecture\n\nThe test infrastructure is organized into three main layers:\n\n### 1. **Validation Helpers** (`test_helpers.py`)\n\nLow-level validation functions for HTTP responses:\n\n- **Status Validation**: `validate_http_status()`, `validate_success()`, `validate_client_error()`, `validate_server_error()`\n- **Content-Type Validation**: `validate_content_type()`, `validate_json_content_type()`, `validate_xml_content_type()`\n- **CORS Header Validation**: `validate_cors_headers()`, `validate_cors_allow_origin()`, `validate_cors_specific_origin()`\n- **HTTP Request Helper**: `make_http_request()` for making test requests with error handling\n\n### 2. **Error Response Fixtures** (`fixtures/error_scenarios.py`)\n\nPre-configured error response fixtures for common HTTP error scenarios:\n\n- **Client Errors (4xx)**: 400 Bad Request, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 405 Method Not Allowed, 415 Unsupported Media Type\n- **Server Errors (5xx)**: 500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable\n- **Generic Builders**: `create_error_response()`, `create_error_batch()` for custom scenarios\n\n### 3. **Test Tables** (`test_tables.py`)\n\nStructured test case management for data-driven testing:\n\n- **Pre-configured Tables**: Common error scenarios, authentication tests, validation tests\n- **Table Builders**: `create_auth_test_table()`, `create_not_found_test_table()`, `create_rate_limit_test_table()`\n- **Execution Helpers**: `run_test_case()`, `run_test_table()` for running tests\n- **Extensible Architecture**: Easy to create custom test tables for any error type\n- **Extension Examples**: Comprehensive examples for S3, database, network, and business logic errors\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n### Fixture Immutability\n\nError fixtures use immutable builder patterns:\n\n```python\nfixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users\")\n# Returns new fixture, doesn't modify original\nwith_path = fixture.with_path(\"/api/items\")\n```\n\n**Why**: This prevents accidental state mutations in parallel tests and makes test behavior predictable.\n\n### Tuple-Based Response Format\n\nValidation functions accept both `requests.Response` objects and simple tuples:\n\n```python\n# With requests.Response\nvalidate_http_status(response, 200)\n\n# With tuple (status, headers, body)\nvalidate_http_status((200, {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, '{}'), 200)\n```\n\n**Why**: Tuples work without the `requests` library, making tests runnable in minimal environments and easier to mock.\n\n### Pattern-Matching Content Types\n\nContent-Type validation uses pattern matching:\n\n```python\n# Both of these match 'application/json'\nvalidate_content_type(response, 'application/json')\nvalidate_content_type(response, 'application/json; charset=utf-8')\n```\n\n**Why**: Real-world responses often include charset parameters. Pattern matching makes tests more robust without complex logic.\n\n### Collection-Based Fixtures\n\nFixtures are organized in collections by category:\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES, SERVER_ERROR_FIXTURES\n```\n\n**Why**: Enables bulk testing against all client errors or all server errors without maintaining manual lists.\n\n## Quick Start Examples\n\n### Validating HTTP Status Codes\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status, validate_client_error\n\n# Single status code\nvalidate_http_status(response, 200)\n\n# Multiple acceptable codes\nvalidate_http_status(response, [200, 201, 204])\n\n# Check for client error\nvalidate_client_error(response)\n```\n\n### Using Error Fixtures\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture, get_fixture\n\n# Create a 404 fixture\nfixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/123\")\nassert fixture.status_code == 404\n\n# Get pre-configured fixture\nfixture = get_fixture('404_not_found')\nassert fixture.status_code == 404\n```\n\n### Making Test Requests\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_helpers import make_http_request\n\nresponse = make_http_request(\n    base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n    path='/api/health',\n    method='GET'\n)\n\nif response['success']:\n    print(f\"Status: {response['status_code']}\")\n    print(f\"Body: {response['body']}\")\n```\n\n## Testing Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Fixture-Based Testing\n\nUse pre-configured fixtures for standard error scenarios:\n\n```python\ndef test_404_not_found():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found response structure.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/123\")\n    \n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n    validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Table-Based Testing\n\nUse test tables for data-driven scenarios:\n\n```python\ndef test_not_found_multiple_paths():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 response for multiple path types.\"\"\"\n    table = create_not_found_test_table([\n        TestCase(path=\"/api/users/999\", expected_code=\"not_found\"),\n        TestCase(path=\"/api/items/abc\", expected_code=\"not_found\"),\n        TestCase(path=\"/api/orders/xyz\", expected_code=\"not_found\"),\n    ])\n    \n    results = run_test_table(table)\n    assert all(result.passed for result in results)\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Helper-Based Validation\n\nUse validation helpers for custom test logic:\n\n```python\ndef test_custom_error_response():\n    \"\"\"Test custom error response with specific fields.\"\"\"\n    response = make_http_request('http://localhost:8080', '/api/error')\n    \n    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 418)\n    validate_content_type(response['headers'], 'application/json')\n    \n    # Custom validation\n    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n    assert body['error'] == 'custom_error'\n    assert 'details' in body\n```\n\n## Adding New Error Test Cases\n\nWhen adding new error test cases, follow these steps:\n\n1. **Choose Your Approach**:\n   - Use existing fixtures for standard errors (404, 500, etc.)\n   - Create custom fixtures with `create_error_response()` for new scenarios\n   - Use test tables for multiple similar scenarios\n\n2. **Write the Test**:\n   ```python\n   def test_my_new_scenario():\n       fixture = create_error_response(\n           status=418,\n           error_type=\"im_a_teapot\",\n           message=\"I'm a teapot\",\n           code=418\n       )\n       \n       validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 418)\n       validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n   ```\n\n3. **Run and Verify**:\n   ```bash\n   pytest tests/test_my_new_scenario.py -v\n   ```\n\nSee [TEST_PATTERNS.md](TEST_PATTERNS.md) for detailed guidance on adding new test cases.\n\n## Extending Test Tables\n\nThe test table framework is designed to be easily extensible for custom error types and scenarios. You can create specialized test tables for:\n\n- **Protocol-specific errors** (S3, WebSocket, gRPC)\n- **Infrastructure errors** (database, network, filesystem)\n- **Business logic errors** (validation, state transitions, constraints)\n- **Domain-specific errors** (financial, healthcare, e-commerce)\n\n### Quick Example\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase, ErrorTestTable\n\ndef create_s3_error_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    return ErrorTestTable(\n        name=\"s3_errors\",\n        description=\"S3 protocol-specific error scenarios\",\n        test_cases=[\n            TestCase(\n                id=\"S3-001\",\n                description=\"Bucket not found\",\n                input_data={\"operation\": \"ListObjects\", \"bucket\": \"nonexistent\"},\n                expected_status=404,\n                expected_error=\"NoSuchBucket\",\n                expected_message=\"The specified bucket does not exist\"\n            )\n        ]\n    )\n```\n\n### Extension Documentation\n\n- **[TEST_TABLE_EXTENSION_GUIDE.md](TEST_TABLE_EXTENSION_GUIDE.md)** - Comprehensive guide for creating custom test tables\n- **[test_table_extensions_examples.py](test_table_extensions_examples.py)** - Complete working examples for various error types\n- **[TEST_TABLE_QUICKSTART.md](TEST_TABLE_QUICKSTART.md)** - Quick reference for common patterns\n\n### Available Extension Examples\n\nThe `test_table_extensions_examples.py` file includes complete implementations for:\n\n1. **S3 Errors** - Bucket/object operations, ACL issues, multi-part upload errors\n2. **Database Errors** - Connection issues, constraint violations, transaction deadlocks\n3. **Rate Limiting** - Token buckets, sliding windows, per-user/per-IP limits\n4. **Network Errors** - Timeouts, DNS failures, SSL/TLS errors, connection resets\n5. **Filesystem Errors** - Permission issues, disk full, file not found\n6. **Business Logic Errors** - State transitions, concurrent modification, rule violations\n\nEach example includes the test table definition and a demo executor function.\n\n## Module Organization\n\n```\ntests/\n├── README.md                           # This file\n├── TEST_PATTERNS.md                   # Guide for adding new test cases\n├── __init__.py                         # Package exports\n├── example_test.py                     # Usage examples\n├── test_helpers.py                     # Low-level validation helpers\n├── test_error_response_validation.py  # Error response structure validation\n├── test_tables.py                      # Test table management\n├── test_http_validation_helpers.py    # HTTP-specific validation helpers\n├── test_error_scenario_fixtures.py    # Tests for error fixtures\n└── fixtures/\n    ├── __init__.py                     # Fixture exports\n    ├── error_scenarios.py              # Error response fixtures\n    └── README.md                       # Fixture documentation\n```\n\n## Exported Functions\n\nThe `tests` package exports the following public API:\n\n### From `test_helpers`:\n- `validate_http_status()` - Validate HTTP status codes\n- `validate_content_type()` - Validate Content-Type headers\n- `validate_cors_headers()` - Validate CORS headers\n- `make_http_request()` - Make HTTP requests with error handling\n\n### From `test_error_response_validation`:\n- `validate_error_response()` - Validate error response structure\n- `validate_standard_error_response()` - Validate standard error format\n- `validate_detailed_error_response()` - Validate detailed error format\n\n### From `test_tables`:\n- `TestCase` - Test case dataclass\n- `TestTableResult` - Test table execution results\n- `run_test_table()` - Execute a test table\n- `create_simple_test_table()` - Create a simple test table\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- [TEST_PATTERNS.md](TEST_PATTERNS.md) - Detailed guide for adding new test cases\n- [fixtures/README.md](fixtures/README.md) - Error fixtures documentation\n- [TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md](TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md) - Test tables usage guide\n- [internal/validate/TYPES_DOCUMENTATION.md](../internal/validate/TYPES_DOCUMENTATION.md) - Error type documentation\n\n## Contributing\n\nWhen contributing to the test infrastructure:\n\n1. **Add Examples**: New functions should include usage examples in docstrings\n2. **Update Tests**: Add tests for new validation helpers\n3. **Document Decisions**: Explain non-obvious design decisions in comments\n4. **Follow Patterns**: Use existing patterns for consistency\n\n## Bead Tracking\n\nThis documentation is part of bead `bf-qxhx20` - It defines extensible patterns for testing\nspecific error types and serves as the base for all error test modules.\n\nArchitecture:\n    TestPattern: Base pattern definition for test cases\n    ErrorTestSuite: Container for organizing multiple test tables\n    PatternExecutor: Executes test patterns with validation\n\nUsage:\n    from tests.error_tests.base import TestPattern, ErrorTestSuite\n\n    # Define a test pattern\n    pattern = TestPattern(\n        name=\"not_found\",\n        description=\"Test 404 Not Found errors\",\n        setup=lambda: not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\"),\n        execute=lambda fixture: validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404),\n        validate=lambda result: result.status_code == 404\n    )\n\n    # Create a test suite\n    suite = ErrorTestSuite(name=\"Error Tests\")\n    suite.add_pattern(pattern)\n\nExtension:\n    To extend for specific error types:\n    1. It provides a template for running tests with consistent\n    setup, execution, and validation phases.\n\n    Attributes:\n        name: Unique identifier for this pattern (e.g., \"not_found\", \"unauthorized\")\n        description: Human-readable description of what this pattern tests\n        category: Category of error (e.g., \"client_error\", \"server_error\", \"auth\")\n        setup: Optional setup function that prepares test fixtures or data\n        execute: Function that executes the test and returns a result\n        validate: Function that validates the execution result\n        cleanup: Optional cleanup function to run after execution\n        enabled: Whether this pattern is enabled (disabled patterns are skipped)\n        timeout_ms: Optional timeout for pattern execution in milliseconds\n        tags: Optional list of tags for categorization\n        metadata: Optional dict for additional pattern metadata\n\n    Example:\n        >>> pattern = TestPattern(\n        ...     name=\"not_found\",\n        ...     description=\"Test 404 Not Found errors\",\n        ...     category=\"client_error\",\n        ...     setup=lambda: not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\"),\n        ...     execute=lambda fixture: validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404),\n        ...     validate=lambda result: result.status_code == 404\n        ... )\n    \"\"\"\n\n    name: str\n    description: str\n    category: str\n    setup: Optional[Callable] = None\n    execute: Optional[Callable] = None\n    validate: Optional[Callable[[Any], Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        \"\"\"Validate test pattern after initialization.\"\"\"\n        if not self.name:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test pattern must have a name\")\n        if not self.description:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test pattern must have a description\")\n        if not self.category:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test pattern must have a category\")\n\n    def with_tags(self, *tags: str) -> 'TestPattern':\n        \"\"\"Return a new pattern with additional tags.\"\"\"\n        new_tags = list(set(self.tags + list(tags)))\n        return TestPattern(\n            name=self.name,\n            description=self.description,\n            category=self.category,\n            setup=self.setup,\n            execute=self.execute,\n            validate=self.validate,\n            cleanup=self.cleanup,\n            enabled=self.enabled,\n            timeout_ms=self.timeout_ms,\n            tags=new_tags,\n            metadata=self.metadata.copy()\n        )\n\n    def with_metadata(self, **metadata) -> 'TestPattern':\n        \"\"\"Return a new pattern with additional metadata.\"\"\"\n        new_metadata = {**self.metadata, **metadata}\n        return TestPattern(\n            name=self.name,\n            description=self.description,\n            category=self.category,\n            setup=self.setup,\n            execute=self.execute,\n            validate=self.validate,\n            cleanup=self.cleanup,\n            enabled=self.enabled,\n            timeout_ms=self.timeout_ms,\n            tags=self.tags.copy(),\n            metadata=new_metadata\n        )\n\n    def execute_pattern(self) -> 'PatternResult':\n        \"\"\"Execute this test pattern and return the result.\"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n        status = PatternStatus.RUNNING\n        error_message = None\n        result_data = None\n\n        try:\n            # Skip if disabled\n            if not self.enabled:\n                return PatternResult(\n                    pattern=self,\n                    status=PatternStatus.SKIPPED,\n                    execution_time_ms=0,\n                    error_message=\"Pattern is disabled\"\n                )\n\n            # Setup phase\n            setup_result = None\n            if self.setup:\n                setup_result = self.setup()\n\n            # Execution phase\n            if self.execute:\n                result_data = self.execute(setup_result) if setup_result is not None else self.execute()\n\n            # Validation phase\n            if self.validate and result_data is not None:\n                is_valid, validation_msg = self.validate(result_data)\n                if not is_valid:\n                    status = PatternStatus.FAILED\n                    error_message = f\"Validation failed: {validation_msg}\"\n                else:\n                    status = PatternStatus.PASSED\n            else:\n                # No validation, consider passed if no exception was raised\n                status = PatternStatus.PASSED\n\n            # Cleanup phase\n            if self.cleanup:\n                self.cleanup()\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            status = PatternStatus.ERROR\n            error_message = str(e)\n\n        finally:\n            execution_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000  # Convert to ms\n\n        return PatternResult(\n            pattern=self,\n            status=status,\n            result_data=result_data,\n            error_message=error_message,\n            execution_time_ms=execution_time\n        )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PatternResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Result of executing a test pattern.\n\n    Contains the outcome, execution time, and any error messages from\n    running a test pattern.\n\n    Attributes:\n        pattern: The test pattern that was executed\n        status: The status of the execution (passed, failed, error, skipped)\n        result_data: Optional data returned from the execution phase\n        error_message: Optional error message if execution failed\n        execution_time_ms: Time taken to execute the pattern in milliseconds\n    \"\"\"\n\n    pattern: TestPattern\n    status: PatternStatus\n    result_data: Optional[Any] = None\n    error_message: Optional[str] = None\n    execution_time_ms: Optional[float] = None\n\n    @property\n    def
